taskDescription: Click on the key anatomical terms related to muscle in the text below.
textField:
There are three types of muscle: *skeletal*, *cardiac*, and *smooth*. *Skeletal* muscle is *voluntary*, has *striations*, and *multinucleated* fibres with *peripheral* nuclei. *Cardiac* muscle is *involuntary*, *striated*, and has *branching* fibres connected by *intercalated* *discs*. *Smooth* muscle is *involuntary*, *non-striated*, and has a *single* *central* nucleus.

Muscle attachments include *tendons* (round, cord-like) and *aponeuroses* (flat, sheet-like). A *spurt* muscle inserts far from the joint as a *prime* *mover*, while a *shunt* muscle inserts close to the joint as a *stabiliser*.

The functional unit of *skeletal* muscle is the *sarcomere*, bounded by *Z* lines. It contains thick *myosin* and thin *actin* filaments. Contraction occurs at the *neuromuscular* junction where *acetylcholine* is released from the motor nerve terminal.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review muscle features and try again.
Good effort! Review the areas you missed.
